title: Pain In Pleasure by Elizabeth Barrett Browning — Love Poetry Lines
content:
A THOUGHT ay like a flower upon mine heart,
And drew around it other thoughts like bees
For multitude and thirst of sweetnesses;
Whereat rejoicing, I desired the art
Of the Greek whistler, who to wharf and mart
Could lure those insect swarms from orange-trees
That I might hive with me such thoughts and please
My soul so, always. foolish counterpart
meaning: "Pain In Pleasure" by Elizabeth Barrett Browning is a love and deep and nature poem consisting of 14 lines. The poet explores themes of love and deep and nature through vivid imagery and emotional depth. Beginning with "A THOUGHT ay like a flower upon mine heart, And drew around it other thoughts like bees...", this piece captures the essence of human experience and invites reflection on the deeper currents of life and feeling. This work is part of Elizabeth Barrett Browning's celebrated body of poetry that continues to resonate with readers worldwide.
